---
name: Counterexample or falsification
description: Record a mathematical, numerical, or empirical failure
title: "Falsification: "
labels: []
assignees:
  - artistso
---

## Claim ID

Identify the exact ledger claim.

## Claim statement and assumptions

Copy the exact statement and every assumption under which it is supposed to hold.

## Counterexample or rejecting result

Provide the smallest valid example, dataset slice, proof, or experiment that rejects the claim.

## Verification

- Exact inputs:
- Expected result under the claim:
- Observed or derived result:
- Reproduction command:
- Artifact paths:
- Environment or toolchain revision:

## Assumption check

Explain why the counterexample satisfies the claim's stated domain. A failure outside the stated domain is a boundary case, not a falsification.

## Scope of rejection

State whether the result rejects:

- the exact theorem;
- one parameter regime;
- an implementation;
- an empirical generalization;
- an interpretation rather than the mathematics.

## Required ledger action

- [ ] Keep current status and narrow assumptions.
- [ ] Mark `falsified`.
- [ ] Mark `retracted`.
- [ ] Add a boundary-case artifact without changing status.

## Preservation rule

Do not delete the rejected claim or failed artifact. Preserve the history, correction, and replacement relationship.
